bead.title: benchmark page: add repeatable 390px responsive verification
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The benchmark pages need a repeatable 390px responsive verification because review of `fizeau-a0c58cbf` could not verify the manual acceptance criterion. Workers need an automated or deterministic local check before the layout bead can be closed.

CURRENT STATE
The affected pages are:
- `/benchmarks/terminal-bench-2-1/`
- `/benchmarks/terminal-bench-2-1/models/`
- `/benchmarks/terminal-bench-2-1/harnesses/`
- `/benchmarks/terminal-bench-2-1/providers/`
The benchmark CSS lives in `website/assets/css/custom.css` and the pages use `.br-body`, `.bench-comparison`, and `.bench-readout` wrappers.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a repeatable local verification script or test that renders those four pages at 390px width and fails on page-level horizontal overflow or obvious text overlap, while allowing intentionally scrollable benchmark tables/charts. Prefer an existing website test harness if one exists; otherwise add the smallest focused test/probe needed.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- `website/` test or script files needed for responsive verification
- `website/assets/css/custom.css` only for small fixes required to make the verification pass
- benchmark page content only if a markup hook is required for the check

NON-SCOPE
- Broad benchmark visual redesign
- Regenerating benchmark data
- Expensive external benchmark runs
bead.acceptance:
1. `cd website && go test ./... -run TestBenchmarkPagesFitAt390px -count=1` passes; the test or equivalent named probe covers the four benchmark pages at 390px width and fails on page-level overflow or obvious text overlap outside intentionally scrollable tables/charts.
2. `cd website && go test ./... -count=1` passes.
3. `cd website && hugo --quiet` passes.
